overview: Temur Delver is a traditional Legacy aggro-tempo deck that has returned to prominence following the December 2024 banlist. It relies on a low mana curve, a heavy cantrip suite, and early threats like Delver of Secrets to apply immediate pressure. The deck aims to disrupt opponents with Wasteland and Lightning Bolt while using Questing Druid for card advantage to outlast attrition wars.
win_conditions: Flipping Delver of Secrets on turn 2 for early damage
Dealing direct damage to finish the game
Using Murktide Regent's Delve to cast large threats late game
Generating card advantage with Questing Druid to outlast opponents

keep_criteria: Hands with 1-2 lands and proactive action (creatures or cantrips)
Hands with a mix of cantrips, one creature, and one interaction
Hands that can open with Ponder to find a second land safely
mulligan_criteria: Hands that are too reactive without guaranteed pressure
Hands lacking action or card draw

examples.reason: Too reactive and Bauble alone can't guarantee pressure.
Provides turn 1 action, high chance to transform Delver, and enables Murktide Regent's Delve if resolved.
Ideal hand structure; open with Ponder to draw a land and avoid enemy Wasteland.

early_game: Play cheap threats like Delver of Secrets on turn 1 or 2
Use Lightning Bolt and Daze to disrupt early threats and protect your board
Cast Wasteland immediately to hinder opponent's mana base
mid_game: Trade cards efficiently in attrition wars against blue tempo decks
Use Questing Druid to generate card advantage and stabilize
Leverage cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to find answers and threats
late_game: Close out games with direct damage or remaining creatures
Cast Murktide Regent using Delve if early threats are removed
Rely on sideboard answers like Consign to Memory or Price of Progress against specific archetypes

key_cards.name: Delver of Secrets
key_cards.notes: Flipped on turn 2 to deal early damage and apply pressure.

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Key early game interaction that became relevant again after Psychic Frog's ban.

key_cards.name: Wasteland
key_cards.notes: Used to stop opponents from developing mana, particularly Eldrazi Temples and Ancient Tombs.

key_cards.name: Questing Druid
key_cards.notes: Fills the role left by banned Expressive Iteration to help win attrition wars.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Part of the heavy cantrip suite that enables the deck's low mana curve and hand quality.

key_cards.name: Murktide Regent
key_cards.notes: Becomes playable via Delve if early Delvers are resolved by the opponent.

sequencing_tips: Open with Ponder instead of Delver when holding only one land to safely draw a second land and avoid enemy Wasteland.
Prioritize destroying the opponent's basic Island as soon as possible when facing Red Stompy to mitigate Blood Moon.
Keep Daze in hand against Red Stompy even if they play Simian Spirit Guide, as it remains important when playing first.
Watch for Chalice of the Void in Eldrazi matchups and adjust mana usage accordingly.

matchups.advice: Focus on stopping them from reanimating cards by swapping removals for sideboard answers like Grafdigger's Cage.
Expect a war of attrition where you trade card for card; Questing Druid helps recover card disadvantage.
Use Wasteland to hit Eldrazi Temple, Eye of Ugin, or Ancient Tomb. Lightning Bolt is excellent against their smaller creatures.
Resolve Blood Moon and Chalice of the Void quickly. Destroy their basic Island ASAP when playing first.
Play as aggressively as possible to prevent them from assembling their combo pieces.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: The December 2024 banlist brought back from the shadows one of the most traditional Legacy decks: Temur Delver... you're still playing an aggro deck.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: flipping Delver on turn 2 is still one of the most efficient tactics around
finish the game by dealing damage to what's left of their life points directly
if your opponent manages to deal with the 2 Delvers, this will most likely enable Murktide Regent 's Delve
adopted Questing Druid , and, in some lists, Up the Beanstalk , as card advantage

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: you should open with Ponder instead of Delver to draw a second land and not risk running into enemy Wasteland s
go after your basic Island as soon as possible; doing this is easier when you're playing first
They can play around Daze with Simian Spirit Guide , but it is still important when you're playing first, so don't send them back straight away
You should keep your eyes open for Chalice of the Void

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Psychic Frog dominated the format... made Lightning Bolt basically irrelevant
Lists with black are dangerous because of Orcish Bowmasters
stops Thought-Knot Seer 's and Sowing Mycospawn 's triggered abilities
Nomads en-Kor and Nadu, Winged Wisdom , are resistant to Lightning Bolt
They play 2 threats you have to resolve... Blood Moon and Chalice of the Void
Your counters might lose value because of Cavern of Souls
They can play around Daze with Simian Spirit Guide
